data.description:
Provide tooling to capture verbose session logs without hand-assembling them: a
convenience capture helper and an automatic per-session hook.

## Why

The requester wants all three content approaches. Beyond plain
`create`/`edit`/`comment`, agents should have (a) a helper to append session
activity into a log ticket, and (b) an auto-session hook that creates/updates a
session log per agent session so logging is effortless and consistent.

## What

- Capture helper: a command/function that creates a `session_log` (if needed) and
  appends activity to it, wiring through the same shared write path.
- Auto-session hook: integrate with session lifecycle so a `session_log` is
  created at session start and updated as work proceeds, with a title carrying a
  short work summary (per the documented convention, not enforced).
- Allow optional `relates_to` / `discovered_from` links from the log to the work
  it documents.

## Scope

Capture/automation layer only; relies on the foundation, performance isolation,
and the recent-logs command. The title-summary convention is documented (not
enforced) in the docs story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A capture helper can create a `session_log` and append entries to it from
      all three interfaces (or library + CLI at minimum, with MCP parity noted).
- [ ] The auto-session hook creates exactly one `session_log` per session and
      updates it without manual steps.
- [ ] Logs may carry `relates_to` / `discovered_from` links; no blocking links.
- [ ] Tests cover helper append and hook create/update; no graph/health impact.
data.title: Session-log capture helper and auto-session hook
